rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', resolve the first unresolved identifier on the same line or remove the invalid clause.
Ensure AGREE scalar type names match AADL expectations: int, real, bool for constants and equation declarations.
Align helper declaration types with the types of the expressions they represent to avoid assumed vs actual type mismatches.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
AGREE references must resolve to visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes that are not declared in the AADL model or its configured dependencies.
Do not add architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics unless the missing references are valid AADL features.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about annex placement
